executive_summary: The Iran-US conflict has reached a critical inflection point with the confirmed assassination of Iran's Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei in a joint US-Israeli covert operation in Tehran. This killing of Iran's top leader, along with seven senior IRGC officials, fundamentally changes the command structure of Iranian forces and marks the conflict's most significant escalation. Three US soldiers have been confirmed killed in Iranian retaliatory strikes (Kuwait and Erbil), and European powers France, Britain, and Germany have now formally joined the coalition with defensive military measures. The conflict spans ten countries with 750 incidents in 24 hours.
whats: Khamenei assassination confirmed - Israeli intelligence with CIA support killed Iran's Supreme Leader and seven senior IRGC officials in multi-location Tehran operation (19:27:25+00:00)
Three US service members confirmed killed in Iranian strikes on Kuwait base (Camp Buehring) - first US fatalities directly attributed to Iranian direct attack (19:17:28+00:00)
European powers formally join coalition - France, Britain, Germany announce 'defensive measures' against Iranian military capabilities (20:13:26+00:00)
UK grants base access - Starmer allows US to use two British military bases for defensive strikes on Iranian missile sites
Maersk halts all Suez and Hormuz transits - major shipping disruption citing escalating security risks from widening US-Israeli war on Iran
confirmed_events: IRGC launches ninth wave of 'True Promise 4' operation targeting Israeli positions and American military assets across the region (19:30:42+00:00)
Iranian ballistic missiles strike Tel Aviv, damaging approximately 200 buildings with mass evacuations (19:59:14+00:00)
Iranian missiles strike Jerusalem area (at least 7 wounded), West Bank settlement Maaleh Adumim - expansion into Palestinian territories (20:42:21+00:00)
IRGC claims targeted US carrier Abraham Lincoln with four cruise missiles in Southeastern Indian Ocean, causing vessel to flee mission area (19:58:18+00:00)
Baghdad airport attacked - NATO Eurofighter jets struck by missiles/drones; three suicide drones hit Victoria NATO base (Islamic Resistance Iraq reporting)
unverified_emerging: Explosion reported at British RAF Akrotiri base in Cyprus - Israeli Red Alert system detected possible drone strike (source: War Monitors, Israeli Red Alert system)
Gandhi Hospital in Iran struck by US-Israeli airstrike - nurses evacuating newborn babies (single source: multiple Telegram channels)
Unconfirmed reports Iran attacked British base in Cyprus - if verified would be major escalation (source: War Monitors)

people_travel: UAE closes embassy in Tehran, withdraws all diplomatic staff - complete diplomatic severance
Gulf airports continue closures with airspace restrictions across region
Baghdad International Airport targeted - major aviation disruption in Iraq
supply_chain: Maersk suspends ALL vessel transits through Suez Canal corridor, Strait of Hormuz, and Bab el-Mandeb Strait - complete shipping halt in both critical chokepoints
Port shutdowns across Gulf region - Salman Port in Bahrain attacked, UAE logistics disrupted
Supply chain disruption described as 'most widespread since pandemic'
market_macro: Oil markets in flux as Hormuz transit halted - significant impact on global oil supply chain
Gulf businesses reel from regional shutdowns - three killed in business disruption across Gulf states
US missile stockpiles depleting faster than anticipated - constraint on sustained operations (Daily Mail)

outlook_24_72h.base_case: The conflict enters a new phase following Khamenei's assassination. Iranian command and control faces potential disruption, but IRGC forces remain operational and will likely continue retaliatory strikes. Trump has stated operations will continue until all objectives achieved, with US officials suggesting a 4-week timeline. Expect continued Iranian proxy attacks across Iraq, Syria, Lebanon, and against Israel. European coalition expansion strengthens US position. Iranian military options are limited but will continue high-volume but largely ineffective strikes.
escalation_risks: Khamenei death triggers Iranian national mobilization - potential for irrational escalation
Russian warning issued (Senator Pushkov) about potential for full-scale war - Moscow involvement risk
If Cyprus base attack confirmed - UK formally enters conflict, dramatically expanding scope
US carrier targeting may draw America into direct naval confrontation with Iran
Nuclear facility targeting - Israel has approved plans to continue operations against Iran
outlook_24_72h.de_escalation_pathways: Oman confirms diplomatic door remains open, but Iranian Foreign Minister states Iran will determine timing and end of war. With Khamenei dead, succession struggle within Iranian leadership may create opportunity or chaos. However, Trump administration appears committed to military solution within 4-week window, making de-escalation unlikely in near term. Iranian defiance statement suggests no pause in hostilities regardless of leadership loss.
